mitre_attack: T1556.006 - Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication
T1550.001 - Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
cwe: CWE-347 - Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

threat.title: SAML Response Manipulation via XML Signature Wrapping in Spring Boot and ASP.NET Core
threat.description: XML Signature Wrapping (XSW) attacks allow attackers to manipulate SAML assertions while maintaining valid XML signatures, enabling authentication bypass and privilege escalation. By carefully crafting a SAML response with duplicate assertions or injecting unsigned content, attackers can impersonate any user, including administrators, in enterprise SSO implementations. This vulnerability exploits the semantic gap between XML signature validation and application business logic processing.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker authenticates with valid low-privilege credentials to the Identity Provider (IdP)
2. Intercepts legitimate SAML response using browser proxy (Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP)
3. Crafts malicious SAML assertion with administrative privileges while preserving original signed assertion
4. Wraps unsigned assertion around signed content, exploiting XML parser ambiguity
5. Replays modified SAML response to Service Provider (SP), bypassing signature validation
6. Gains unauthorized administrative access, exfiltrates sensitive data, or modifies critical business logic
prerequisites: Application validates XML signature but processes different assertion tree
Weak or missing reference validation in SAML response processing
No additional integrity checks (e.g., assertion ID binding, timestamp validation)
Attacker has valid credentials or can intercept SAML traffic

cve_references.description: XML signature wrapping vulnerability in GitHub Enterprise Server SAML authentication allowing unauthorized access without prior authentication
cve_references.affected_versions: GitHub Enterprise Server prior to 3.14, fixed in 3.13.3, 3.12.8, 3.11.14, 3.10.16

cve_references.description: Ruby-SAML XML signature wrapping attack allowing bypass of SAML authentication and unauthorized access by exploiting digital signature verification flaws
Spring Security SAML2 service provider signature wrapping vulnerability allowing malicious users to modify valid SAML responses and append arbitrary assertions
cve_references.affected_versions: Spring Security 5.2.x prior to 5.2.4, 5.3.x prior to 5.3.2

real_world_breaches.incident: Azure Active Directory SAML Persistence Flaw
real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers could retain access to SAML applications after user assignment removal, enabling persistent unauthorized access
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Vulnerability in Azure AD SAML token generation allowed users to maintain access after permissions revoked

real_world_breaches.impact: Critical CVSS 9.0 vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and generate REST API tokens for Azure AD and SAML accounts
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Authentication flaw in FME Server 2021.2-2022.2 and FME Flow 2023.0 with imported Azure AD/SAML accounts

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Rocket.Chat SAML authentication bypass via XML signature wrapping

security_research.title: Every Signature is Broken: On the Insecurity of Microsoft Office's OOXML Signatures
security_research.key_finding: Universal signature forgery attacks demonstrating XML signature wrapping vulnerabilities persist across modern implementations, including SAML tokens

security_research.title: Ruby-SAML Pwned by XML Signature Wrapping Attacks
security_research.key_finding: Detailed analysis of CVE-2024-45409 demonstrating how XML canonicalization and signature validation gaps enable authentication bypass

mitigations.title: Implement Strict XML Schema Validation with Reference Checking
mitigations.description: Enforce strict XML schema validation ensuring the signed reference ID matches the processed assertion ID. Use hardened SAML libraries that validate the entire signature reference chain and prevent XML structure manipulation.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Verify assertion signature is validated independently from response signature
Test with modified SAML assertion to confirm rejection
Check reference ID matching between signature and assertions
Test timestamp validation with expired assertions

mitigations.title: Configure ASP.NET Core with Hardened SAML Signature Validation
mitigations.description: Implement comprehensive SAML signature validation in ASP.NET Core using Sustainsys.Saml2 or ComponentSpace libraries with strict XML canonicalization and assertion binding validation.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Install Sustainsys.Saml2 NuGet package (v2.x or later)
Configure WantAssertionsSigned = true in IdP settings
Test with tampered SAML response to verify rejection
Validate InResponseTo binding with request ID
Enable application insights for SAML validation failures

mitigations.title: Deploy Runtime SAML Response Monitoring and Anomaly Detection
mitigations.description: Implement comprehensive logging and monitoring of SAML authentication events with anomaly detection for suspicious patterns including duplicate assertions, mismatched timestamps, privilege escalations, and geographic anomalies.

code_example.code:
# Azure Sentinel KQL Query for SAML Authentication Anomalies

# Query 1: Detect Multiple Assertions in SAML Response (XML Wrapping Indicator)
let samlLogs = AppLogs
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend AssertionCount = toint(Properties.AssertionCount)
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend SourceIP = tostring(Properties.ClientIP);

samlLogs
| where AssertionCount > 1
| summarize Count = count(), IPs = make_set(SourceIP) by Username, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count > 3
| project TimeGenerated, Username, AssertionCount, IPs, Severity = "High"
| extend AlertDescription = strcat("Potential XML wrapping attack: User ", Username, " with multiple assertions")

# Query 2: Detect Privilege Escalation via SAML
let baselineRoles = AppLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend Roles = parse_json(Properties.Roles)
| summarize PreviousRoles = make_set(Roles) by Username;

AppLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend CurrentRoles = parse_json(Properties.Roles)
| join kind=inner (baselineRoles) on Username
| where CurrentRoles has "admin" and not(PreviousRoles has "admin")
| project TimeGenerated, Username, PreviousRoles, CurrentRoles, IPAddress = Properties.ClientIP
| extend Severity = "Critical", AlertType = "SAML Privilege Escalation"

# Query 3: Detect Timestamp Manipulation
AppLogs
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend AssertionTimestamp = todatetime(Properties.NotBefore)
| extend ServerTimestamp = TimeGenerated
| extend TimeDrift = abs(datetime_diff('second', AssertionTimestamp, ServerTimestamp))
| where TimeDrift > 300 // More than 5 minutes drift
| project TimeGenerated, Username = Properties.Username, AssertionTimestamp, ServerTimestamp, TimeDrift
| extend Severity = "Medium", AlertType = "SAML Timestamp Anomaly"

# Query 4: Geographic Impossibility Detection
let userLocations = AppLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend Location = geo_info_from_ip_address(tostring(Properties.ClientIP))
| extend Country = tostring(Location.country), City = tostring(Location.city)
| summarize LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), LastCountry = any(Country), LastCity = any(City) by Username;

AppLogs
| where OperationName == "SAML_Authentication"
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend CurrentLocation = geo_info_from_ip_address(tostring(Properties.ClientIP))
| extend CurrentCountry = tostring(CurrentLocation.country)
| join kind=inner (userLocations) on Username
| where CurrentCountry != LastCountry
| where datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastSeen) < 60
| project TimeGenerated, Username, LastCountry, CurrentCountry, TimeSinceLastAuth = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastSeen)
| extend Severity = "High", AlertType = "SAML Geographic Impossibility"

# Query 5: Signature Validation Failures
AppLogs
| where OperationName == "SAML_SignatureValidation"
| where Properties.ValidationResult == "Failed"
| extend FailureReason = tostring(Properties.Reason)
| extend Username = tostring(Properties.Username)
| extend IPAddress = tostring(Properties.ClientIP)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), Reasons = make_set(FailureReason) by Username, IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where FailureCount > 5
| project TimeGenerated, Username, IPAddress, FailureCount, Reasons
| extend Severity = "Critical", AlertType = "SAML Signature Validation Attack"

# Create Analytics Rule
# Name: SAML XML Wrapping Attack Detection
# Severity: High
# Tactics: Initial Access, Credential Access
# Frequency: 5 minutes
# Query Period: 10 minutes

nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are aggregated and correlated
validation_steps: Configure application to log SAML assertion count to Azure Log Analytics
Deploy KQL queries as scheduled analytics rules
Create alert action groups for critical SAML anomalies
Test detection with simulated XML wrapping attack
Validate geographic impossibility detection with VPN testing

indicators: SAML responses containing multiple assertions with same Subject
Signature reference IDs not matching processed assertion IDs
Sudden privilege escalation (user to admin) via SAML authentication
SAML assertions with timestamps outside acceptable clock skew
Authentication from geographically impossible locations within short timeframe
Repeated SAML signature validation failures from same IP
SAML InResponseTo field missing or not matching original AuthnRequest ID
User sessions with conflicting identity attributes

log_queries.query:
index=webapp sourcetype=saml_auth
| eval assertion_count=mvcount(assertion_id)
| where assertion_count > 1
| stats count by user, client_ip, assertion_count, _time
| where count > 3
| eval severity="HIGH"
| eval description="Potential SAML XML wrapping attack - Multiple assertions detected"
| table _time, user, client_ip, assertion_count, severity, description

sequence by user.name with maxspan=5m
  [authentication where event.outcome == "success" and saml.assertion_count > 1]
  [authentication where event.outcome == "success" and user.roles:"admin" and 
   not user.previous_roles:"admin"]

fields @timestamp, username, sourceIP, assertionCount, roles
| filter operationName = "SAMLAuthentication"
| filter assertionCount > 1 or (roles like /admin/ and previousRoles not like /admin/)
| stats count(*) as anomalyCount by username, sourceIP, bin(5m)
| filter anomalyCount > 2
| sort @timestamp desc
